enonce.texte: Match each modal verb with its correct function.

questions.texte: Identify the modal verb used to express ability in the present.
Identify the modal verb used to ask for permission in a polite way.
Identify the modal verb used to express prohibition.
Explain the difference between 'can' and 'could' when expressing ability.
questions.reponse: 'Can' is used for present ability, while 'could' is used for past ability or polite requests.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct modal verb: can, could, cannot (can't), could not (couldn't).

questions.texte: When I was young, I ________ run very fast.
She ________ speak three languages fluently now.
We ________ go to the party because we were busy.
I ________ play the piano very well when I was a child.
She ________ finish the project on time. She worked very hard.

enonce.texte: Choose the correct modal verb to complete each sentence: can, may, could, can't, mustn't.

questions.texte: ________ I use your phone, please? (polite request)
You ________ smoke here. It's forbidden.
Students ________ leave the classroom without permission.
________ I come in? (informal request)
You ________ talk during the exam. It's against the rules.

enonce.texte:
Read the school rules below. Then answer the questions using modal verbs of ability, permission, or prohibition.

School Rules:

  • Students must arrive at school before 7:30 AM.
  • Students cannot use mobile phones in class.
  • Students may eat during break time only.
  • Students can borrow books from the library.
  • Students must not cheat during exams.

questions.texte: Analyze: What is the rule about arriving at school? Express it using a modal verb.
questions.reponse: Students must arrive at school before 7:30 AM.

questions.texte: Analyze: Can students use their phones in class? What modal verb expresses this prohibition?
questions.reponse: No, they can't. Students cannot use mobile phones in class.

questions.texte: Create a sentence asking permission to borrow a book from the library.
questions.reponse: May I borrow a book from the library? / Can I borrow a book from the library?

questions.texte: Create a sentence giving advice to a student who wants to cheat.
questions.reponse: You must not cheat during exams. / You shouldn't cheat.

questions.texte: Compare: What is the difference between 'must not' and 'cannot'?
questions.reponse: 'Must not' expresses a strong prohibition or obligation to not do something (it's against the rules). 'Cannot' expresses impossibility or lack of permission. Both can be used for prohibition, but 'mustn't' is stronger.

intitule: Writing Practice: School or Workplace Rules

enonce.texte: Write a short paragraph (about 80 words) describing the rules at your school or workplace. Use can, can't, must, mustn't, may, and could at least once each.

questions.texte: Design a short text describing rules using modal verbs.
questions.reponse:
Exemple de réponse:

In our school, students must arrive before 7:30 AM. We can borrow books from the library, but we cannot use our phones in class. We may eat during break time only. Students must not cheat during exams. Could you please respect these rules? The teachers are very strict about them. Everyone must follow the rules to maintain a good learning environment.

enonce.texte: Imagine you are at a restaurant. Write a dialogue between you and the waiter using modal verbs of ability, permission, and prohibition.

questions.texte: Create a dialogue between a customer and a waiter using modal verbs.
questions.reponse:
Exemple de réponse:

Waiter: Good evening! May I take your order?
Customer: Yes, please. Can I have the grilled fish?
Waiter: Of course. Could I recommend the house special?
Customer: Sorry, I can't eat spicy food.
Waiter: That's fine. You can have it without spices. You must try our dessert too!
Customer: Thank you! May I pay by credit card?
Waiter: Yes, you can. Customers can pay with cash or credit card.

enonce.texte: Find and correct the errors in the following sentences.

questions.texte: She can to swim very well.
May I to use your phone?
You must not to smoke here.
He could play the piano when he was young?
questions.reponse: Could he play the piano when he was young? (pour une question) ou He could play the piano when he was young.

questions.texte: They can't to come to the party tomorrow.
questions.reponse: They can't come to the party tomorrow.

enonce.texte:
Read the text and answer the questions.

Working in a Modern Office

In our company, employees can work from home on Fridays. They can choose their own working hours as long as they complete their tasks. However, they must attend meetings in the office on Monday mornings. Employees cannot use the office computers for personal reasons. They may use their personal phones during lunch break. They must not share confidential information with others. Could you please follow these rules carefully?

questions.texte: Analyze: What can employees do on Fridays?
questions.reponse: Employees can work from home on Fridays.

questions.texte: Evaluate: What must employees do on Monday mornings?
questions.reponse: Employees must attend meetings in the office on Monday mornings.

questions.texte: Analyze: What cannot employees use office computers for?
questions.reponse: Employees cannot use office computers for personal reasons.

questions.texte: Create: Write a polite question to ask for permission to use the office phone.
questions.reponse: May I use the office phone, please? / Could I use the office phone?

questions.texte: Compare: What is the difference between 'must not' and 'cannot' in the context of the office?
questions.reponse: In this context, 'must not' (must not share confidential information) expresses a strong obligation or rule that is strictly prohibited. 'Cannot' (cannot use computers for personal reasons) expresses a rule or lack of permission. Both are prohibitions, but 'mustn't' is used for very important rules that could have serious consequences.
